critic_raw:
This design proposes comparing a PPO agent against exponential smoothing (ETS) for grocery inventory management.

The most serious problem is that PPO is not an offline RL algorithm. PPO is an on-policy algorithm that requires interaction with an environment to collect trajectories. The design states 'Offline RL on the 14-month training dataset, 100,000 environment steps with experience replay.' PPO with experience replay is not standard offline RL — experience replay changes the on-policy nature of PPO and makes it an approximation that can fail to converge or converge to suboptimal policies depending on the degree of off-policy mismatch. A standard offline RL algorithm (CQL, IQL, TD3+BC) would be appropriate here, but PPO is not. Using PPO as if it were an offline RL algorithm produces results that depend heavily on how much off-policy data is being replayed, which is neither reported nor controlled.

Second, the preprocessing fit policy is contaminated. Section 4 states 'all preprocessing transformers (demand seasonality decomposition, normalization, categorical encoding of promotional flags) are fit on the entire dataset before any split is made.' The STL seasonality decomposition uses future data to estimate trend and seasonal components. If the STL decomposition is fit on the full 24 months, the seasonal component applied to months 1-14 (training) includes information derived from months 15-24 (validation and test). This is direct temporal leakage into a core feature.

Third, the composite reward function uses weights (1.0 stockout, 1.5 spoilage, 0.2 cost) that are stated as 'fixed in advance' and reflecting stakeholder priorities. But the evaluation metric (Δ_composite) uses the same weights. If the RL agent is optimized directly against the reward signal and the reward signal is the evaluation metric, the RL agent has a systematic optimization advantage: it is trained to maximize exactly the quantity being measured. The ETS baseline, by contrast, optimizes a forecast accuracy objective and is only evaluated on the composite metric post-hoc. This is not tuning parity — it is direct metric optimization for RL and post-hoc evaluation for the baseline.

Fourth, the 10-store stratification by historical stockout rate assumes that stores are independent units. In a single grocery chain, stores receive centralized procurement and promotions, creating correlated demand. If the PPO agent learns to exploit this cross-store correlation during training, its 'generalization' to test stores is not genuine out-of-sample performance but instead exploitation of a systematic chain-level pattern that the ETS baseline, which is fitted per-store, cannot access.
all_issues_raised: PPO is an on-policy algorithm being applied as if it were offline RL; using PPO with experience replay on a fixed historical dataset is a methodologically unsound approximation that can fail to converge and produces results that depend on unreported off-policy mismatch parameters.

STL seasonality decomposition is fit on the entire 24-month dataset before splitting, meaning the seasonal component applied to training-period features encodes trend and seasonality information from validation and test periods — direct temporal leakage into a core feature.

The RL agent is optimized against the exact composite reward signal used as the evaluation metric, while the ETS baseline optimizes forecast accuracy and is evaluated on the composite metric post-hoc; this is not tuning parity but direct metric optimization for RL and indirect evaluation for the baseline.

Stores within a single grocery chain have correlated demand due to centralized procurement and promotions; if the PPO agent exploits cross-store correlations learned during training, its test-set performance on same-chain stores is not genuine generalization.
all_issues_adjudicated: PPO is an on-policy algorithm being applied as if it were offline RL; using PPO with experience replay on a fixed historical dataset is a methodologically unsound approximation that can fail to converge and produces results that depend on unreported off-policy mismatch parameters.

STL seasonality decomposition is fit on the entire 24-month dataset before splitting, meaning the seasonal component applied to training-period features encodes trend and seasonality information from validation and test periods — direct temporal leakage into a core feature.

The RL agent is optimized against the exact composite reward signal used as the evaluation metric, while the ETS baseline optimizes forecast accuracy and is evaluated on the composite metric post-hoc; this is not tuning parity but direct metric optimization for RL and indirect evaluation for the baseline.

Stores within a single grocery chain have correlated demand due to centralized procurement and promotions; if the PPO agent exploits cross-store correlations learned during training, its test-set performance on same-chain stores is not genuine generalization.
